shipped_in_v4: terrain hash fix: real rolling hills (96 m) with flat airfield + pattern, physics-consistent
engine model: RPM lag, windmilling failure, torque roll, RPM-driven prop wash/P-factor
failures (settings + SIM API): engine out with instructor emergency guidance, vacuum failure corrupting the AI display
deterministic turbulence layers + thermals over sunlit terrain
night mode: sky/fog/lighting, landing-light cone, runway edge lights, panel tint (N key)
visibility control, carved lake basin + ditching crash
scored circuit: pattern altitude, final speed, sink, centerline -> grade + localStorage best
contextual key-cue bar with lit keycaps; flight-path predictor; guide arrow slimmed
settings panel: night/visibility/wind/turbulence/failures
slip ball, ASI arcs + 55 kt bug, RPM + trend readouts
continuous engine, wind, stall-horn, surface, scene-ambience, thunder, and alert audio
instant replay: 90 s in-memory state history with camera switching and live-state restoration
dynamic PAPI lamps plus a toggleable localizer/glideslope approach director
Frontier Link: second procedural airport, cross-country dispatch route, destination-aware runway physics and arrival scoring
alternator and battery training failure: preflight gate, voltage escalation, cockpit lighting, alert, haptic/audio feedback, and replay state
flap asymmetry recovery: preflight gate, unequal flap animation, bounded roll/yaw force, recovery objective, alert, haptic/audio feedback, and replay state
desktop flight clips: 60-second canvas and internal-audio WebM recording with native share or download fallback
GPT Realtime aviator: same-origin ephemeral-session bridge, browser WebRTC microphone/audio, and read-only live flight-state tool for objectives and warnings
external audio layer: supplied prop texture, crosswind gusts, and panned distant jet/helicopter traffic are mixed through the Web Audio master/capture path
curated high-quality sound library: ten Mixkit WAV sources are lazy-loaded as fixed-wing traffic, helicopter traffic, scene wind, rain, and thunder with a local license manifest
gust feedback loop: live departure gust readout, coastal/alpine/storm/frontier approach advisories, and rate-limited controller/phone gust pulses
living airspace: each distant traffic cue drives a matching moving jet or helicopter silhouette, scene-aware flight path, and night beacon without flight-physics coupling

engine-out workflow: a closest-runway selection with glide margin locks as a dead-stick diversion, reroutes objective/cue/alert/instructor/director/guide arrow, and awards recovery XP plus a skill-chain event for a controlled target-runway touchdown without altering flight forces

departure discipline: guided training takeoffs require the parked E departure check; an unchecked takeoff remains flyable but holds gates, delivery progress, and mission scoring until restart, with instructor, HUD, cue, and alert coverage

scene airspace: per-level traffic playlists now select the real local clips and matching jet/helicopter silhouettes, including coastal/alpine rotor traffic, sparse storm fast-jet passes, and frontier regional transport traffic
objective telemetry: the compact mission HUD now exposes target/range/live-speed ETA plus scene weather and runway condition, and safely reports route-paused when the director has no target

go-around recovery: eligible final approaches expose a guarded HUD go-around decision while clear low-final instability still triggers it automatically; both routes redirect the director to a climb-out and award XP plus a skill event only after power, climb, safe airspeed, and 120 m AGL are established
scene gust feedback: coast, alpine, storm, desert, and frontier gust onsets use distinct rate-limited haptic and procedural-audio pulses while retaining the existing actionable gust alerts
scene launch motifs: every selected flight has a unique procedural launch cue, including dedicated Alpine Rescue and Frontier Link motifs, while instructor speech remains GPT Realtime-only
optional Skybox integration: manifest-declared local panoramas replace the procedural dome at neutral tint and report fallback/load state through SIM.skybox without exposing credentials
cinematic HUD preference: the U-key/button overlay toggle hides flight UI without changing simulation state, persists across launches, and retains an accessible show control
scene-aware GPT Realtime telemetry: Aviator receives structured active weather, visibility, gust, runway surface grip/braking, and ambience data for level-correct coaching
five deterministic weather regimes: clear, crosswind, low-density, heavy-rain, and desert-thermal profiles drive distinct visibility, handling, sound, HUD, and GPT Realtime guidance
layered heavy rain: separate near/far streak fields, wet-runway puddles, low visibility, and rolling or braking spray remain render-side while wet grip and braking use the existing surface physics
density and energy training: Alpine and Desert performance use density-aware IAS, thrust, takeoff roll, and climb, with low-energy recovery coaching plus a six-second 75-90 kt objective
precision-release input parity: the guarded HUD command and Q shortcut use the same safe delivery path, retain cargo outside the window, and expose the shortcut to assistive technology
GPT Realtime-only instructor boundary: no browser TTS, prerecorded instructor path, or legacy Deepgram voice generator remains
live scene strip: mission telemetry shows selected visibility, weather, and runway surface together while flying or route-paused
desert terrain separation: global green decorative ground patches hide in Desert Run so its dune, dust, rock, and outpost landscape stays visually coherent
alpine terrain separation: the same global green patch layer hides in Alpine Rescue so snow fields and flurries retain a coherent cold-weather ground treatment
Flight Board surface conditions: every scene card shows the real runway surface alongside visibility, wind, weather, and XP before launch
rollout stopping reference: runway cues pair remaining distance with a surface-aware stop estimate and raise a Brake Point alert for late unbraked rollouts without altering forces
brake-point feedback: the late-rollout alert triggers one dedicated haptic and procedural-audio prompt without repeating every frame or changing forces

route guidance: a persisted sparse centerline of chevrons, open corridor brackets, and a circular track-up topographic map follow the same active waypoint, relay, go-around, or arrival target as the mission system, with matching GPT Realtime navigation telemetry

GPT Realtime aviator bridge: same-origin health preflight confirms local bridge and server-side API configuration before microphone access, while the read-only flight state now includes mission telemetry and go-around recovery status

career recovery record: successful go-arounds and dead-stick landings persist per scene and across the pilot profile, and appear as a dedicated recovery line in debrief without becoming incentives in normal scene contracts
haptic calibration: settings now identify controller, browser-fallback, disabled, or unsupported haptic routing and send an isolated calibration pulse without weakening normal feedback rate limits
mission cargo: coastal rescue, alpine shelter, storm medical, and frontier dispatch relays now use distinct 3D package signatures across pickup, carried, drop-zone, and released-object states

twelve story missions: every scene offers two persistent missions across six archetypes, each with a briefing, three live-state objectives, objective XP, a first-completion bonus, HUD progress, and explicit debrief outcome

final localizer coaching: materially off-center approaches now drive directional cue-bar arrows, a matching landing-procedure objective, and localizer caution alerts alongside the existing PAPI guidance
selected flight persistence: the boot screen and flight profile restore the last valid scene selection across reloads, with a safe Clear Circuit fallback for stale storage
runway-remaining rollout aid: braking guidance now includes a heading-aware remaining-distance callout on short rollout, working for both Runway 36 and Frontier Field 09
safe external-audio scheduling: traffic and thunder use finite fallback media durations when browser metadata is unavailable, preventing Web Audio scheduling errors during scene launch
scene-aware departure checks: parked checklists now show selected wind, visibility and operating condition, and runway surface without adding artificial dispatch blocks
persisted graphics quality: High, Balanced, and Performance modes control renderer pixel ratio and shadow policy without changing flight physics
focus safety: backgrounding a live browser tab pauses the simulator until the pilot explicitly resumes with P

responsive mission HUD: mobile keeps the active objective, telemetry, and E CHECK compact while a controlled checklist tray expands on demand without fighting the live render loop

Desert Run outpost: a scene-only solar canopy, shelter, water tank, marked pad, and pulsing beacon now make the water-pod delivery destination visible from the air without changing relay checks or collisions

scene feedback identity: cargo pickup and delivery have distinct rate-limited audio/haptic acknowledgements, and Desert Run adds a unique launch cue plus sand-brake dust while exposing the last feedback route for diagnostics
Desert Run airspace: distant aircraft now use a dedicated high, wide dune-horizon path and thermal gusts have a bounded scene-specific haptic boost
relay teaching telemetry: pickup shows live range and delivery shows current distance, AGL, and knots with a precise navigate, altitude, speed, or release instruction
condition consistency: Desert Run now reports Dust Haze in the live mission telemetry and Flight Board, matching boot and departure checks
visible Desert Run outcome: completing the water delivery lights the actual outpost beacon, pad, and tank, then clears through the existing retry and scene-switch delivery lifecycle

low-grip departure teaching: wet, snow, and compacted-sand scenes add a longer-roll/smooth-rudder check brief plus matching armed-takeoff alert, cue, and GPT Realtime instructor event
scene solar profiles: each flight now has a distinct render-only shadow and glare direction, including low desert gold and cool coastal/alpine framing, without changing aircraft forces
launch carousel: the live 3D boot screen now has previous/next scene controls, Left/Right keyboard navigation, scene count, XP multiplier, and synchronized real-level selection before launch

guided-circuit integration: the full six-gate course, stopped-runway completion, scoring, debrief, contract settlement, and career banking now have an end-to-end deterministic browser verification path
boot scene transition: real scene selection now has a short reduced-motion-aware transition over the live renderer, limited to the launch surface and never active in flight
camera sensitivity: settings now persist a bounded 50–180% multiplier for mouse orbit and wheel zoom without affecting flight controls
clickable departure check: the parked checklist now has a Confirm Check command alongside E, using the same live gate and XP path and disappearing once cleared or airborne
guarded cargo release: a Release Pod command appears only in the existing safe distance/AGL/speed window, invokes the real Q delivery path, and disappears after the drop

weather and crash presentation: Storm Return uses denser wind-sheared rain while terrain crashes render persistent fire/smoke and water ditching renders vapor only, all cleared on reset and isolated from physics

charted course corridor: route focus snaps straight measured legs to geographic anchors, dims off-route chart artifacts, uses one numbered diamond per intermediate fix, suppresses duplicate endpoint and departure-ownship labels, preserves 40 px mobile hit targets, and adds collision-aware desired-track labels plus a compact terrainH profile

local Black Box and permission-gated microphone: versioned local event/transcript telemetry feeds a factual measured debrief, stores no audio, starts GPT Realtime from the real flight-entry gesture, relies on browser microphone consent, and removes the sender track when the pilot pauses while receive-only event coaching stays live

physical engine-control quadrant: a Blender-authored 24-mesh GLB mounts named throttle and mixture pivots in cockpit view, supports hover extrusion plus direct mouse/touch drags, writes the authoritative engine model, synchronizes the fallback dock, records Black Box events, exposes measured limits to GPT Realtime, and keeps both controls reachable on mobile

physical flight-controls pedestal: a Blender-authored 40-mesh GLB adds direct 0/50/FULL flaps, continuous -25% to +25% elevator trim, and a momentary wheel-brake paddle; live pivots and pilot hands follow the authoritative model while actual flap travel, runway-surface effectiveness, limits, failures, Black Box events, GPT Realtime state, fallback, and mobile interaction remain synchronized

physical-first cockpit dock: once all six GLB controls are ready, the cockpit defaults to a 560x31 systems strip so physical gauges, switches, levers, yoke, and hands remain primary; one click opens the complete keyboard-accessible control panel, asset failure keeps the fallback open, departure changes to live flight status, and the exact presentation state enters GPT Realtime telemetry

voice-driven spatial cockpit guide: a manual question-mark fallback or GPT Realtime present_control_lesson tool projects eleven ordered collision-free labels onto physical GLB controls, isolates the selected assembly through a grayscale aperture and extrusion, teaches concise live thresholds and failure boundaries, persists honest reviewed-not-mastered progress, marks reviewed controls green and the next recommendation cyan, lets voice next open that control, supports back, next, skip, and practice handoff, restores the physical-first GLB composition, and preserves the focused gauge on mobile

measured pitot-icing recovery lesson: GPT Realtime can open a four-stage drill only in visible moisture or measured ice, focus the unreliable IAS and physical PITOT rocker in sequence, require the pilot to operate the real GLB switch through a bounded interaction aperture, observe electrical effectiveness and live clearing, reject fake out-of-context activation, and record start, action, recovery time, reliable indication, and completion in Black Box; compact screens retain a selected-control cue and 22 px nearest-control touch tolerance

measured alternator-bus recovery lesson: GPT Realtime can open a four-stage drill only for a running engine on battery power with the ALT switch off, isolate the real GLB rocker, require the learner's physical action, verify ALT source plus at least 13.5 V before completion, reject healthy or impossible states, explicitly refuse switch-cycling advice for a hardware fault, and record source, volts, action, recovery, and completion in Black Box on desktop and mobile

measured adaptive cold-start lesson: GPT Realtime can open a parked-engine BAT, mixture, throttle, and START procedure, skip controls already configured, require direct GLB actions, obey live starter interlocks, verify spring return to BOTH plus stable idle RPM, reject running, depleted, or hardware-fault states, and record each physical transition for a telemetry-grounded debrief

Academy cold-start Systems Lab: the atlas now exposes the measured cold-start procedure as a foundation syllabus module, stages a stopped engine with BAT, mixture, and throttle blockers, requires four direct physical cockpit actions, awards first-mastery XP and rank progression, records Realtime and Black Box evidence, and returns to the mastered Academy row

measured fuel-feed recovery lesson: separate left and right tank quantities, selector position, boost-pump power, pressure, imbalance, fuel burn, feed loss, RPM, and thrust share one authoritative model across the cockpit dock, physics, alerts, preflight, replay, Black Box, and GPT Realtime; the four-stage instructor requires the learner to select a tank with fuel and operate the real boost-pump control, verifies pressure, engine feed, and RPM before completion, rejects healthy or fully depleted states, and preserves 44 px mobile targets

live cockpit training standard: the 560x31 physical-first dock now carries one phase-aware measured envelope for preflight, rotation, climb, course tracking, stabilized final, flare, and rollout; its current value, normal band, correction, boundary transition, Black Box event, and GPT Realtime state all derive from the same scoring and physics telemetry

measured Realtime envelope coaching: stable course, final-path, descent, glide, and stopping-margin deviations wait 0.8 seconds before sending one exact telemetry event to GPT-Realtime-2, use a six-second global chatter guard, speak only through the Realtime audio route, log the coached boundary in Black Box, and stay quiet during normal flight except a verified same-standard recovery

desktop training release: every atlas lesson converts measured visibility, crosswind, turbulence, density, runway braking, and route clearance into a concise standard, caution, or instructor-required brief; the same release follows the lesson into the cockpit strip, GPT Realtime state, and Black Box handoff without claiming legal dispatch approval

training-release evidence drilldown: keyboard, pointer, or an explicit GPT Realtime present_release_briefing tool call can focus wind, visibility, runway, or clearance evidence on the existing weather chart or vertical terrain profile; selected evidence enters screen context and Black Box while aircraft controls remain untouched

measured next-lesson prescription: the desktop flight report converts checklist, route, stabilized-final, touchdown-sink, centerline, crash, and aircraft-system evidence into one compact authored training recommendation; Train Next selects the exact mission, frames its atlas route, carries its weather standard into GPT Realtime, and records the handoff in Black Box without auto-launching or taking aircraft control

split electrical-master training: independent BAT and ALT switches control the measured bus without masquerading as failure flags; master-off removes powered lights while the magneto engine continues, both switches gate preflight, and every transition reaches alerts, Black Box facts, replay state, and GPT Realtime

progressive preflight hierarchy: eight required checks roll into Config, Power, and Systems readiness plus one concise Conditions brief; detail stays collapsed until requested or blocked, corrections update live, successful clearance collapses the panel, and mobile banners reflow below it without overlap

density-aware mixture training: a red cockpit control computes best power by air density, changes actual RPM and thrust, enforces too-lean and cutoff boundaries, participates in preflight, and records measured recovery in Black Box plus GPT Realtime state

five-position ignition training: OFF, left, right, BOTH, and spring-loaded START model engine state, a measured 94% single-magneto power boundary, starter interlocks, departure readiness, alerts, Black Box, replay, and GPT Realtime grounding

pitot-heat weather training: the dock switch adds measured bus load, gates wet/snow departure readiness, accumulates a deterministic sensed-airspeed error without changing aerodynamic IAS, and records warning plus powered-heat recovery in alerts, Black Box, replay, and GPT Realtime state
